folio.label: 181r
folio.l: And to oure heigúe goddes , thanke we
Of honour æ oure eldres , witú vs laft
¢ His wyf , his lordes , & hise concubynes
Ay dronken , while here appetites laste
Out of æeise noble vesseles , sondry wynes
And oon a wal , this kyngö his eyen caste
And sey an hand , armlees , æ wroot ful faste
For fere of whicú , he quoke & siked sore
This hand æ Balthasar , so sore a gaste
Wroot , mane techel phares , & nomore
¢ In al æ lond , Magicien was noon
That coude expoune , what æis lbr;re mente
But Danyel , expouned it a noon
And seide kyngö , god to thy fader sente
Glorie & honour , regne , tresor , Rente
And he was proud , & nothyngö god ne dradde
And æäfore , god , gret wreche vp oon hi~ sente
And hi~ bireft , the regne that he hadde
¢ He was out cast , of ma~nes compaignye
Witú Asses , was his xx habitaciou~
And ete hey as a beest , in wete & drye
Til æ he knew by gce , & by resou~
That god of heuene , hatú domynacou~
Ouer euäy regne , and euery creature
And æa~ne had god , of hi~ compassiou~
And hi~ restored his regne , & his figure
¢ Eke æè æ art his sone , art proud also
And knowest alle æeise thynges , verrely
And art rebel to god , and art his foo
Thow drank eke of hise vessels , boldely
Thy wyf eke , and æine wenches synfully
Dronke of the same vesselles , sondry wynes
And heriest false goddes , cursedly
Therfore to the , I shapen , ful gret peyne is
¢ This hand was sent fro god , æ oon the wal
Wroot , Mane thechel phares , truste me
Thy regne is don , æè weyest nougút at al
Dyuyded is thy regne , and it shal be
To Medes and to îses , I ©euen q¶ he
And thilke same nygút this kyngö was slawe
And Darius occupietú , his degree
Thougú he æä to had , neiæä rigút ne lawe
¢ Lordynges ensample , her by moun ©e take
How æ in lordship~ , is no sykernesse
